don: noun. A university professor, particularly one at Oxford or Cambridge.
dor: noun. Any of species Geotrupes stercorarius of large European dung beetles that make a droning noise while flying.
duo: noun. Two people who work or collaborate together as partners; especially: Two musicians who perform music together.
durr: interjection. Alternative form of duh (“indicating stupidity etc.”).
nod: verb. To incline the head up and down, as to indicate agreement.
nor: conjunction. And... not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
nous: noun. The mind or intellect, reason, both rational and emotional.
ons: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of on
oud: noun. A short-necked and fretless plucked stringed instrument of the lute family, of Arab and Turkish origin.
our: determiner. Belonging to us, excluding the person(s) being addressed.
ours: pronoun. That or those belonging to us.
rod: noun. A straight, round stick, shaft, bar, cane, or staff.
round: adjective. Of shape: Circular or cylindrical; having a circular cross-section in one direction.
sod: noun. The stratum of the surface of the soil which is filled with the roots of grass, or any portion of that surface; turf; sward.
sorn: verb. to impose upon another for food and lodging.
sou: noun. An old French copper coin equal to one twentieth of a livre or twelve deniers; one sou is to the livre as one shilling is to the
sudor: noun. Sweat; the salty fluid excreted by the sweat glands.
sun: noun. A star, especially when seen as the centre of any single solar system.
surd: noun. An irrational number, especially one expressed using the √ symbol.
surround: verb. To encircle something or simultaneously extend in all directions.
undo: verb. To reverse the effects of an action.
urn: noun. A vase with a footed base.
dur: adjective. Major; in the major mode.
rond: noun. A bank of a river or a canal.
roun: noun. A speech or conversation (especially rumoured or whispered).
ruds: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rud
sond: noun. Alternative form of sonde (“testing device”).
sud: noun. A bubble of lather or foam (the singular of suds).
sur: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mwaghavul.
unsour: verb. To remove the sourness from; to make no longer sour.
urs: noun. The anniversary of the death of a Sufi saint, celebrated with music and ritual.
urson: noun. A species of New World porcupine, Erethizon dorsatum.
